business.type_label: portrait photographer (pregnancy/family/beauty/business), owner of Wolf Studios

engine.ladder_reasoning: A photographer whose portfolio (17 filterable images, gallery pages, photo-heavy hero) IS the proof the visitor evaluates before booking; the priced packages and studio/hours support the decision but the visual work is what is browsed.

goal.one_action: book a photography session via the on-site 4-step booking wizard (secondary: send a contact-form message / call)

structure.archetype_note: Nav exposes 7 pages; rendered capture recovered /contact (real form + Google Maps embed + hours) and /book (4-step booking wizard: PAKKET / STUDIO & DATUM / GEGEVENS / BEVESTIGING). Prices and Studio pages not crawled; package/pricing content also appears inline on home.

mapped.observed_as: The Essence Session / The Signature Experience / The Full Experience / Business & Branding
Portfolio teaser + filterable Portfolio page (17 items)
Wat Klanten Zeggen (2 client testimonials w/ Instagram handles)
Klaar Om Jouw Verhaal Te Vertellen?
About me (bio narrative, owner of Wolf Studios)
CONTACTGEGEVENS + OPENINGSTIJDEN + ONZE LOCATIE (address, hours, Google Maps embed)

unmapped.observed: /book 4-step booking wizard (PAKKET → STUDIO & DATUM → GEGEVENS → BEVESTIGING) with per-package SELECTEER and date/studio selection
unmapped.why_no_fit: No canonical section id represents a multi-step, stateful on-site booking/scheduling flow. It is not a leadForm (single submit) nor a pricing table (it selects date + studio inventory and confirms a booking). The section vocab assumes static persuasion sections, not an interactive booking engine.
The primary work-proof surface for a work-engine site, but no dedicated 'portfolio/gallery' section id exists; socialProof understates it as the core decision surface.

proposed_new.name: booking-wizard
proposed_new.evidence: /book is a confirmed on-site 4-step wizard (package selection → studio & date scheduling → customer details → confirmation) with priced ALL-IN packages (€495/€1495/€2495). No existing capability (lead-form, packages) expresses stateful multi-step booking with date/studio inventory selection.

confidence.why: fetch_quality is 'rendered'; /contact (form + map + hours) and /book (booking wizard structure + step 1 packages) were recovered via headless render. Only wizard steps 2-4 (behind interaction) and design hints (fonts/colors empty) remain uncaptured — neither changes the classification.

gap_findings: PRIMARY GAP: the confirmed on-site 4-step booking wizard (package → studio & date → details → confirmation) is a booking engine with scheduling/inventory selection. It exceeds the 'one conversion action' ICP definition and no existing capability or section id can express it — hence in_icp:false. 'lead-form' and 'packages' both undershoot it; proposed capability 'booking-wizard'.

The site actually offers TWO conversion paths: a simple /contact lead-form (which alone WOULD be in-ICP) and the dominant /book booking wizard (out of scope). The primary CTA everywhere ('Boek Een Sessie') routes to the wizard, so the site's real job is the booking engine.
A dedicated filterable portfolio/gallery page — the primary proof surface for a work engine — has no canonical section id and maps awkwardly onto socialProof.

Explicit price ladder (€495/€1495/€2495 + no-price business tier) on a work-engine photography site blends work-proof with trust-style package selling; 'packages' capability covers the display but not the wizard that consumes it.
